entities.description: bookrag is a retrieval system evaluated for its performance against other baselines
m3docvqa is a dataset used to evaluate the retrieval recall of bookrag
graphranker is a layout based baseline system compared against bookrag
the ift inspired selector   reasoner workflow is the process used by bookrag to classify queries and analyze information
agent based planning is a component of the workflow that classifies the query
the selector is a component that narrows the search to a precise information patch
the reasoner is a component that performs analysis on the selected information
skyline ranker is a process that retains a specific number of nodes after analysis
71 2  is the retrieval recall achieved by bookrag on the m3docvqa dataset
44 5  is the maximum retrieval recall achieved by the graphranker baseline
9 87 is the average number of retained nodes on one of the three datasets after the skyline ranker process
6 86 is the average number of retained nodes on another of the three datasets after the skyline ranker process
8 6 is the average number of retained nodes on the third dataset after the skyline ranker process
10 is the value of k used in the standard top k setting for comparison
retrieval performance is the specific metric being evaluated to validate the retrieval design of bookrag
retrieval recall is the specific performance metric used to compare bookrag against other baselines
ground truth layout blocks are the reference data used to evaluate the retrieval recall
layout based baselines are the group of systems against which bookrag is compared
the query is the input that is classified by the agent based planning component
the information patch is the precise data segment targeted by the selector component
candidate size is the variable representing the number of candidates  which is kept from inflating by the skyline ranker process
three datasets are the collective group of data used to measure the average number of retained nodes
the standard top k setting is the baseline configuration used for comparison with the skyline ranker results

relations.description: bookrag achieves a 71 2  recall on the m3docvqa dataset
bookrag significantly outperforms graphranker in retrieval recall
the performance advantage of bookrag stems from its ift inspired selector   reasoner workflow
the workflow includes agent based planning which classifies the query
the workflow uses the selector to narrow the search to a precise information patch
the workflow uses the reasoner for analysis after the selector
the skyline ranker process results in an average of 9 87 retained nodes on one dataset
the skyline ranker process results in an average of 6 86 retained nodes on another dataset
the skyline ranker process results in an average of 8 6 retained nodes on the third dataset
the number of retained nodes by skyline ranker is comparable to the standard top k setting where k 10
bookrag s retrieval performance is the subject of the validation described in the text
bookrag s retrieval recall is the specific metric measured to demonstrate its performance
bookrag is evaluated against ground truth layout blocks to validate its design
bookrag is evaluated against layout based baselines to demonstrate its superiority
the workflow s agent based planning component classifies the query
the selector narrows the search to a precise information patch
the skyline ranker process ensures high quality retrieval without inflating the candidate size
the average number of retained nodes by skyline ranker is measured across three datasets
the results of the skyline ranker process are compared to the standard top k setting
